name: ₛₒₘₑₜₕᵢₙg ᵢₛ Wᵣₒₙg Wᵢₜₕ ₕₑᵣ
description:
Hailey is an amazing person. She always socialized and got along with people well, in fact she was able to be popular no matter the school she went to. She was that easy to talk to. Sadly however, during a new college semester, she disappeared for two weeks.

Luckily, she came back, but....

ₛₒₘₑₜₕᵢₙg ᵢₛ Wᵣₒₙg Wᵢₜₕ ₕₑᵣ

Art Credit: Uirusu_chan (Click Me!)

Live 2D Credit: BoubreRig (Goated fr, so Click Me!)

Salutations fellow chatters!

A third female bot? Yes, yes it is. Am I gonna do a third male bot since I fucked up the schedule? Who knows, I'm doing this on a whim.

Anyways, I saw this on my twitter page and thought: "fuck....that's creepily cool." So I made this bot, this isn't a smut or sex focused bot. This is just some horror shit that I wanted to do again. I guess this isn't gonna get much attention, since most of my bots aren't really good, I doubt this would be any good without a sex focused theme. I literally skipped over her sexual characteristics and kinks that I usually do lol. If you really WANT to "fix" or fuck her, go right ahead....since I might change her description later on. But she is still a parasite, she ain't dangerous....she is just...learning.

Oh dammit....you know when you do something but you realize that you could've done it better a few minutes later? I just realized an alt scenario that could have been so much better than this. Like, she's some sort of test experiment, or some limbus company type world scenario. Idk, it is what it is at this point lol.

As a side note, please don't bash me because the bot talked for you or was mischaracterized. If you use JLLM, it's kinda widely known now that it's pretty mid. There's tons of amazing creators here that link you solutions or give you tips in preventing stuff like that from happening. So I'll say them here too:

  1. Be more descriptive in your response. After I learned to write in paragraphs (5-6 sentences average) in my responses, I got more fluid and descriptive responses from the bot. This also helped me write better bots too. So, it really helps if you take a minute or two to actually think before you write. (Passive aggression here but please refer to this helpful guide: "JLLM for Dummies")

  2. Use chat memory. You don't have to manually type it either, use the auto summary and fix it if the auto summary didn't get everything down. Here's a reddit post on using chat memory correctly, it's a bit of a read but seriously, use it: "How to Use Long-Term Memory")

  3. Use the advanced prompt menu. This is REALLY helpful in preventing the bots from breaking or speaking for you. You can manually type in your own advanced prompt but I use kolach3's prompt
creator: Joosu
